title: Browser Presigning, POST Policy, and CORS · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs browser presigning, post policy, and cors from browser, Node, TypeScript, AWS SDK v3, S3, CloudFront, security, billing, and stakeholder evidence and challenges one compile-time misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for least-privilege browser upload session after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, create a one-object upload intent with tenant, generated key, expected size, type, checksum, expiry, status, and replay policy before signing.
Use @aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner 3.1087.0 getSignedUrl with an exact PutObjectCommand region, expiry, key, checksum, content type, encryption choice, and required browser-header parity.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, use a POST policy when browser-enforced key, size, metadata, and field conditions are required and validate the completed object independently.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Upload Session Intent
steps.instruction:

content.text: A review TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 9848 during a AWS SDK v3 dependency canary. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to finalize a browser upload intent while separating CORS from IAM and promise completion; keep every external value unknown until a runtime validator accepts it; then change region, retrieval, or transfer cost and defend the revised budget action. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, create a one-object upload intent with tenant, generated key, expected size, type, checksum, expiry, status, and replay policy before signing.
